articles.title: AI-generated face images of emotional expressions
articles.authors: Robin S. S. Kramer, Natália Javorková, Peter R. Clark
articles.abstract: Recently developed artificial intelligence (AI) tools are now capable of generating realistic face images that are indistinguishable from photographs. However, little is known regarding how these synthetic images portray emotional expressions. Here, with only simple prompting, we generated a set of images depicting seven facial expressions using ChatGPT. These images were intermixed with posed facial expression photographs drawn from a standardised database and we collected participants' judgements regarding which emotions were portrayed, along with their rated intensities. Our results showed that synthetic images were more accurately classified as their intended emotions, as well as more intense displays, in comparison with posed photographs. In addition, AI-generated images depicted action units associated with particular emotions to a similar extent to posed photos, although this pattern varied across emotions. Finally, both types of images were correctly classified by ChatGPT with few or no errors. Taken together, these findings provide insight into how AI tools represent, and subsequently depict, human emotions. Further, this approach may facilitate the creation of novel, individually-tailored image sets for use in future research by incorporating more complex and specific prompting.
articles.keywords: Facial expression; Set; Face; Artificial intelligence; Psychology; Image; Action; Expression; Emotional expression
articles.category: Emotion, Facial Expression, AI, Emotion Expression
articles.summary: 本研究使用ChatGPT生成七种情绪的面部表情图像，并与标准摆拍照片进行比较，参与者对图像表达的情绪类型和强度进行判断。结果显示，AI生成的图像在情绪分类准确性和强度评分上均优于摆拍照片，且动作单元表现相似。这些发现揭示了AI工具如何表征人类情绪，并为未来研究提供了新的方法。

articles.title: Under pressure: The role of supplemental work for cross-domain spillover of psychological need frustration
articles.authors: Ferdinand Baierer, Lucas Alexander Maunz, Jürgen Glaser
articles.abstract: Research on supplemental work has yielded mixed findings, linking it to both ill-being (e.g., exhaustion and recovery impairments) and potential benefits (e.g., increased performance and psychological detachment). What remains unclear are the processes that elicit supplemental work and how it affects ill-being. Drawing on self-determination theory (SDT), we develop a dynamic spillover framework that positions supplemental work as a behavior elicited by basic psychological need frustration (NF) in the work domain. We propose that supplemental work carries costs by increasing NF in the non-work domain, thereby disrupting cross-domain need balance and ultimately increasing next-day exhaustion at work. Results from an experience sampling study (141 employees, 2440 observations) supported our hypotheses: within-person increases in work overload predicted subsequent NF in the work domain and supplemental work. Competence frustration, in particular, triggered subsequent supplemental work that was associated with increased NF in the non-work domain and higher next-day exhaustion. These findings add to the notion of a detrimental pathway of supplemental work and highlight the importance of dynamic, cross-domain need frustration processes in understanding its consequences for employee well-being.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Frustration; Work; Spillover effect; Social psychology; Competence; Experience sampling method; Self-determination theory; Burnout
articles.summary: 本研究基于自我决定理论，构建了动态溢出框架，探讨辅助工作在工作域和非工作域间需求挫折传递中的作用。通过经验取样法发现，工作超负荷会引发需求挫折和辅助工作，特别是能力挫折会触发辅助工作，进而增加非工作域需求挫折和次日工作耗竭。

articles.title: More materialistic by escaping experiences: experiential avoidance mediates the link between attachment anxiety and material values
articles.authors: Emrullah Ecer, Agata Gasiorowska, Tobias Otterbring
articles.abstract: People with attachment anxiety frequently experience problems in social relationships and tend to form a strong attachment toward material objects as a substitute for their interpersonal insecurities. However, the underlying mechanism linking attachment anxiety with material values is not well-defined. We propose that anxiously attached people are more prone to avoid thoughts, feelings, and experiences, thereby leading to greater material values. In three correlational studies and a preregistered experiment conducted across Russian, Turkish, Polish, and U.S. samples (N = 1,397), we investigated whether experiential avoidance mediates the link between attachment anxiety and material values. The indirect effect of attachment anxiety on material values through experiential avoidance was consistently significant across all studies. In Study 4, we also demonstrated that inducing attachment anxiety (vs. negative affect) led to a suppression of unwanted experiences, feelings, and thoughts, resulting in more material values. We suggest that interventions focused on secure relationships may enhance anxiously attached individuals' action strategies, potentially reducing their material values, with implications for the literature on attachment styles and material values as well as broader models of social and clinical psychology.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Experiential avoidance; Materialism; Anxiety; Social psychology; Link; Experiential learning; Developmental psychology
articles.summary: 本研究通过三项相关研究和一项实验，探讨了经验回避在依恋焦虑与物质价值观之间的中介作用。在俄罗斯、土耳其、波兰和美国的跨文化样本中，发现依恋焦虑的个体更倾向于回避思想、感觉和经验，从而导致更高的物质价值观。实验部分还表明，诱导依恋焦虑会抑制不想要的经验和感觉，增强物质主义倾向，提示关注安全关系的干预可能有助于减少物质价值观。

articles.title: Emotional resonance and cognitive processing in cross-media art: A psychological analysis of the synthesis of photography and painting in the works of Liu Xiaodong.
articles.journal: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts
articles.publish_info: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts, 2026-05-11
articles.abstract: Works that combine photography and painting present novel challenges for perception. This study identifies the psychological mechanisms underlying emotional resonance and cognitive processing in the perception of Liu Xiaodong's photo-painting synthesis. A comprehensive methodological approach was employed, including 64-channel electroencephalogram recordings, eye tracking, physiological responses, behavioral assessments, and semistructured interviews. The experimental sample consisted of 120 participants: 60 experts (with more than 5 years of formal art education) and 60 novices (with fewer than two gallery visits per year). Participants viewed 12 works by Liu Xiaodong alongside control images under varying experimental conditions (2 × 2 × 3 factorial design). Eye-tracking analysis indicated enhanced information processing: Gaze duration on faces averaged 6,234 ms, whereas transition probability between regions rose to .47 (compared with .34 for photographs and .28 for paintings). Statistical analysis identified four principal components of responses (explaining 87.5% of variance): empathy (34.2%), cognitive effort (22.8%), aesthetic experience (18.4%), and existential reflection (12.1%). The findings inform the design of art-therapy protocols for trauma rehabilitation, supporting a two-phase activation model (initial phase = 3.8 µs, integrative phase = 4.9 µs). For curatorial practice, a minimum viewing duration of 6.2 s is recommended. Presenting guiding questions before viewing enhanced engagement by 38%-57%. Educational programs should foster the ability to discern the dual aspects of photographic documentary realism and the transformative power of painting. Overall, the study advances theories of embodied art perception by documenting physiological responses that correspond to the thematic content of the artworks.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Painting; Cognition; Psychological analysis; Photography; Cognitive psychology; Cognitive science; Information processing
articles.summary: 本研究采用脑电图、眼动追踪等多种方法，考察了刘小东摄影绘画结合作品在观看中的情感共鸣与认知处理机制。结果发现，这类作品能引发较长的面孔注视时间和增强的区域间转换概率，并识别出共情、认知努力等四个主要心理成分。研究为艺术疗法和策展实践提供了实证依据，并推进了具身艺术感知理论。

articles.title: The Persian Author Recognition Test: Exposure to literary and popular fiction differently predicts theory of mind.
articles.journal: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts
articles.publish_info: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts, 2026-05-11
articles.abstract: Storytelling has long been hailed as a critical factor in human evolution, notably facilitating group living by fostering the development of advanced social cognition. Consistently, research in Western cultures has shown that exposure to literary, but not popular, fiction, measured via the Author Recognition Test, predicts performance on advanced theory of mind (ToM) measures. Here we report results from 2 studies that aimed to validate the Persian Author Recognition Test and to examine the differential effects of exposure to literary versus popular fiction on ToM in Iran. Across these studies ( n = 449 and 454), factor analyses confirmed a 2-factor structure distinguishing recognition of literary and popular fiction authors, and multiple regression analyses showed that exposure to literary, but not popular, fiction positively predicted performance on a ToM task. Exposure to literary fiction also positively predicted scores on a self-reported ToM measure, but this was significant only in Study 1. These effects emerged controlling for personality traits and demographic variables. Gender differences favoring females on ToM measures were also observed. These findings extend the link between exposure to literary fiction and ToM to the Persian cultural context and contribute to the growing body of evidence showing the differential effects of different types of fiction on social cognition.
articles.keywords: Persian; Psychology; Literature; Narrative; Linguistics; Social psychology; Popular fiction; Aesthetics; Popular culture
articles.summary: 本研究在波斯文化中验证了作者识别测试，并考察了对文学小说和流行小说的暴露对心智理论的影响。两项研究显示，暴露于文学小说（而非流行小说）能正向预测心智理论任务表现，控制人格和人口变量后依然显著，表明文学小说对社会认知有特定促进作用。

articles.title: Review of The creativity choice: The science of making decisions to turn ideas into actions.
articles.journal: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts
articles.publish_info: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts, 2026-05-14
articles.keywords: Creativity; Psychology; Epistemology; Engineering ethics; Cognitive science; Ideation; Psychological science; Social psychology; Making-of
articles.summary: 摘要缺失，无法生成自动总结。

articles.title: Genetic and environmental factors underlying the associations between attachment styles and mental and physical health.
articles.authors: Keely A Dugan, Jacob J Kunkel, Stefania V Vacaru, R Chris Fraley, Theodore E A Waters, Matthew McGue, Robert F Krueger, Glenn I Roisman
articles.journal: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
articles.publish_info: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology, 2026-05-11
articles.abstract: Research suggests that people who have relatively secure attachment styles tend to experience better mental and physical health across the lifespan (Pietromonaco & Beck, 2019; X. Zhang et al., 2022). Despite the growing body of research linking attachment styles to health outcomes, relatively little is known about why or how these associations arise. The present study analyzed data from 1,377 older adult twins, who completed measures of their general and relationship-specific attachment styles and five health assessments-depressive symptoms, self-reported mental and physical health, physical illnesses, and waist circumference. Leveraging the study's twin design, we estimated the relative contributions of genetic and environmental factors to the associations between attachment styles and health. Our findings suggest that the genetic factors underlying depression are largely overlapping with the genetic factors underlying attachment anxiety (e.g., genetic correlation, ra, = .70 between general attachment anxiety and depression) and show a moderate degree of overlap with the genetic factors underlying attachment avoidance (average ra = .34). In general, genetic and environmental factors each accounted for approximately half of the total phenotypic associations between attachment styles and mental health outcomes. Furthermore, we found moderate genetic correlations between attachment anxiety and physical health outcomes (average |ra| = .30), but little-to-no overlap among the environmental factors that contribute to attachment anxiety and physical health. Thus, the associations between attachment anxiety and physical health outcomes appear to be primarily attributable to a common or shared genetic liability. Attachment avoidance shared weak or near-zero associations with indicators of physical health.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Attachment theory; Anxiety; Developmental psychology; Insecure attachment; Mental health; Physical health; Clinical psychology; Physical activity
articles.summary: 本研究利用双胞胎设计探讨了依恋风格与心理健康及身体健康之间关联的遗传和环境基础。发现依恋焦虑与抑郁症状共享高度的遗传基础，其与身体健康结局的关联主要源于共同的遗传易感性；环境因素在依恋焦虑与心理健康中各贡献约一半，但与身体健康几乎无重叠，而依恋回避与身体健康的关联较弱。

articles.title: Robust associations of emotional intelligence with human flourishing: A second-order meta-analysis.
articles.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
articles.publish_info: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America, Vol. 123, Issue 19, 2026-05-12
articles.abstract: Emotional intelligence (EI) is an umbrella term that has been used to describe i) the ability to reason accurately about emotions and use emotions to augment thought processes, ii) a constellation of traits reflecting social and emotional functioning, or iii) a mix of relevant abilities and traits. Research on EI has expanded considerably since the 1990s, resulting in dozens of meta-analyses on its relationship with various indices of flourishing (e.g., psychological well-being, adaptive traits, and adaptive functioning in the classroom and workplace). In this second-order meta-analysis, we synthesized data from 62 meta-analyses including over 3,000 studies and 1 million participants. Our findings suggest a robust association between human flourishing and an overall index of EI, r = 0.28, 95% CI [0.25, 0.31], as well as separate indices of ability, trait, and mixed EI. Favorable EI associations were found across several outcome categories (e.g., cognitive abilities, psychological adjustment, mental health, workplace outcomes) as well as age and cultural groups. Nevertheless, effect sizes were most pronounced for self-report EI measures, especially when flourishing was likewise measured subjectively. Further, there was substantial heterogeneity of EI-flourishing effect sizes. We recommend that future research identify additional moderators of EI-flourishing associations. We also discuss ongoing debates about the conceptualization of EI and suggest that future work consider alternative EI schemes.
articles.summary: 本研究通过二阶元分析综合了62项元分析数据，发现情绪智力与人类繁荣（如心理福祉、适应性功能）存在稳健的正相关，整体效应量为0.28。该关联跨年龄和文化组别普遍存在，但基于自报测量的效应更为显著。

articles.title: The role of intolerance of uncertainty, trait anxiety, and OCD symptoms in checking behaviour and its associated concomitants.
articles.authors: Anthea Manuel, Sruthi Sridhar, Bronwyn Steenekamp, Belkis Sayin, Nicolo Biagi, Shannon Wake, Jayne Morriss
articles.abstract: ABSTRACChecking behaviours are used to reduce uncertainty and prevent feared outcomes. It remains unclear how traits such as Intolerance of Uncertainty (IU) and Trait Anxiety (TA), alongside Obessive Compulsive Disorder (OCD) symptoms, influence checking behaviour under varying levels of threat and uncertainty. In this study (n = 208), threat (performance score, performance-contingent electric stimulation) and uncertainty (feedback, no feedback) were manipulated during a visual discrimination and checking task. On each trial, participants identified whether two shapes were identical or different. Measures included self-reported urge to check, distress, checking frequency, task accuracy, and corrugator supercilii muscle activity. Lower TA was associated with more checking under high versus low threat, whereas higher TA showed no checking behaviour differences based on threat. Lower TA was unrelated to reaction time, whereas higher TA was linked to slower reactions in low-threat conditions with feedback. Higher IU predicted stronger urges to check and greater distress. Elevated OCD symptoms were associated with similar corrugator responses regardless of feedback availability, whereas lower OCD symptoms showed greater activity when feedback was unavailable. These findings suggest IU, TA, and OCD symptoms relate to different aspects of checking behaviour, though conceptual replication using more reliable and ecologically valid paradigms is needed.
articles.keywords: OCD symptoms; checking; intolerance of uncertainty; trait anxiety
articles.summary: 本研究探讨了不确定性容忍度、特质焦虑和强迫症症状对检查行为的影响。通过实验操纵威胁和不确定性水平，发现低特质焦虑者在高威胁条件下检查更频繁，而高不确定性容忍度预测更强的检查欲望和痛苦感。这些结果表明不同心理特质与检查行为的不同方面相关。

articles.title: A Life, Not a Nameless Victim: The Impact of Victim Photographs on Perceptions of Victims and Guilt Judgments
articles.authors: Hannah J. Phalen, Kristen L. Gittings, Madison Adamoli, Janice Nadler, Jessica M. Salerno
articles.abstract: The identifiable victim effect suggests that images of murder victims taken before their deaths can increase positive perceptions of those victims, potentially influencing jurors' decision-making. We investigated whether viewing pre-mortem photographs of murder victims biased jurors' perceptions of the victim, and consequently their judgments of the defendant. Across three between-subjects experimental studies (total Ns = 2,456), participants who viewed pre-mortem photographs of the victim (vs. did not view) rated the victim more positively. These more positive perceptions, in turn, predicted a higher likelihood of rendering guilty verdicts. Notably, the effect was stronger for White and Black victims than for Latina victims. These findings suggest that even well-intentioned uses of pre-mortem photographs may inadvertently bias jurors and contribute to racial disparities in the administration of justice.
articles.keywords: identifiable victims; legal judgments; perceptions of people; victim race
articles.summary: 本研究考察了观看谋杀受害者生前照片对受害者感知和有罪判决的影响。结果显示，观看照片的参与者对受害者评价更积极，进而更可能做出有罪判决，且这种效应在白人和黑人受害者中比拉丁裔受害者中更强。这表明即使出于善意使用受害者照片，也可能无意中偏袒陪审员并加剧司法中的种族差异。

articles.title: Loneliness and Societal Pessimism: Longitudinal Evidence From Norway
articles.authors: Thijs van den Broek, Jack Lam
articles.journal: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships
articles.publish_info: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships, 2026-05-11
articles.abstract: This study examines the longitudinal association between loneliness and societal pessimism, i.e., the belief that society is in decline and that people are unable to bring about positive change. Using longitudinal data from 4049 respondents in the Norwegian Generations and Gender Survey (GGS-II), we estimate conditional change score models to assess whether baseline loneliness predicts within-person increases in societal pessimism. Loneliness was measured using the six-item De Jong Gierveld scale, and societal pessimism was captured through a 13-item index covering a range of societal concerns. Results adjusted for baseline pessimism and a range of sociodemographic covariates show that loneliness at baseline was associated with greater increases in societal pessimism. These findings underscore the broader societal impact of loneliness, suggesting that it may not only affect individual wellbeing but also shape attitudes toward society and the future.
articles.keywords: Loneliness; Pessimism; Norwegian; Psychology; Baseline; Longitudinal study; Affect; Optimism
articles.summary: 本研究利用挪威纵向数据，分析了孤独感与社会悲观主义之间的关联。研究发现，基线孤独感与个体社会悲观主义的增加显著相关，表明孤独感不仅影响个人情感状态，还可能塑造对社会和未来的消极态度。

articles.title: Attachment Orientation and the Landscape of Nostalgia: Narrative Content and Well-Being Outcomes
articles.authors: Amelia Dennis, Erica G. Hepper, Tim Wildschut, Constantine Sedikides
articles.journal: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships
articles.publish_info: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships, 2026-05-14
articles.abstract: We examined whether the attachment dimensions of avoidance and anxiety predict the content of nostalgic recollections and accompanying well-being benefits. For this purpose, we developed a coding scheme to evaluate written narratives of nostalgic memories across three themes: self, others, affect. In Study 1 ( N = 431), all participants narrated a nostalgic event and completed an attachment orientation measure. Participants lower (vs. higher) on avoidance included more details about others, connection to others, self-expansion, and positive affect. Those lower (vs. higher) on anxiety included less positive and negative affect. In Study 2 ( N = 392), participants were randomly assigned to narrate either a nostalgic or ordinary memory and reported state well-being. Both avoidance and anxiety were associated with other-related narrative content, which in turn related to well-being benefits. Participants lower (vs. higher) on avoidance expressed greater positivity about others and connection in their nostalgic (but not control) narratives. Participants lower (vs. higher) on anxiety expressed less negativity about others in their nostalgic (but not control) narratives. Both dimensions showed indirect effects via these narrative features on well-being in the nostalgia condition. Overall, avoidance was consistently related to others' portrayal in nostalgic narratives, whereas anxiety's influence on nostalgia was less consistent. Attachment insecurity shapes the content of nostalgic narratives and consequent well-being outcomes.
articles.keywords: Narrative; Psychology; Negativity effect; Anxiety; Content; Developmental psychology; Social psychology; Orientation; Coding; Connection
articles.summary: 本研究考察了依恋维度（回避和焦虑）如何预测怀旧叙事的内容及其对幸福感的益处。通过开发编码方案分析叙事中的自我、他人和情感主题，发现低回避者在怀旧叙事中包含更多关于他人的细节、联系和积极情感，而低焦虑者表达较少情感。依恋不安全性通过叙事特征间接影响怀旧条件下的幸福感，表明其塑造了怀旧叙事的内容和情感结果。

articles.title: Child-Focused Reappraisal and Ruminative Reflection: Associations With Parental Health, Adolescent Academic Achievement, and Parent and Adolescent Gender
articles.authors: Gesine Jordan, Elouise Botes, Samuel Greiff, Ziwen Teuber
articles.journal: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships
articles.publish_info: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships, 2026-05-16
articles.abstract: Adolescence is characterized by dynamic biological and emotional changes that influence key developmental tasks (e.g., dealing with academic demands). Parents are critical for their children's emotion regulation development and offer child-focused emotion regulation strategies in challenging situations. To date, child-focused emotion regulation processes in critical achievement situations are not comprehensively understood as questions remain regarding their associations with parental health and adolescents' academic achievement. In addition, the roles of parents' and adolescents' gender in child-focused emotion regulation have yet to be explored. The current cross-sectional study addressed these gaps by applying single-group and multi-group structural equation modeling, as well as latent mean comparisons in a sample of U.S. parents of sixth-ninth graders ( N = 1,304). Higher levels of child-focused reappraisal were related to parents' lower subjective health issues, whereas child-focused ruminative reflection was negatively associated with adolescents' academic achievement. Further analyses indicated that the structural paths linking child-focused emotion regulation, parental health issues, and adolescents' academic achievement differed by parents' gender, but not by children's gender. Surprisingly, fathers were more likely to utilize child-focused ruminative reflection than mothers and reported a negative association between self-focused reappraisal and health issues, which was not found for mothers. Parents were more likely to use child-focused ruminative reflection with male adolescents, who also had lower average academic achievement than female adolescents. No significant differences in child-focused reappraisal were observed by parents' or adolescent's gender. The results highlighted distinct and context-specific associations between child-focused emotion regulation strategies and family correlates.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Developmental psychology; Association; Cognitive reappraisal; Emotional regulation; Expressive Suppression; Clinical psychology; Structural equation modeling; Academic achievement
articles.summary: 本研究通过结构方程模型，探讨了父母使用面向孩子的情绪调节策略（认知重评和反刍性反思）与父母健康及青少年学业成就的关联，并分析了父母和青少年的性别差异。结果发现，认知重评策略与父母较低的主观健康问题相关，而反刍性反思与青少年较低的学业成就相关；这些关联在父母性别上存在差异，父亲更可能使用反刍性反思策略。

articles.title: Love, Death, and Reason for Living: Positive Romantic Relationships Reduce Death Anxiety Through Meaning in Life Among Females
articles.authors: Shiyun Shen, Shijin Sun, Wei Wei, Zhechen Wang
articles.journal: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships
articles.publish_info: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships, 2026-05-16
articles.abstract: While the buffering effect of romantic relationships on existential anxiety has been proposed within terror management theoretical framework, the specific mechanisms through which such relationships mitigate death anxiety remains underexplored. Across three studies ( N s = 138, 130, and 473), we examined whether positive romantic relationships reduce death-thought accessibility by enhancing meaning in life. Results revealed that positive romantic relationships reduced death-thought accessibility (Study 1) and enhanced meaning in life (Study 2). Moreover, mediation analyses indicated that the association between positive romantic relationships and reduced death-thought accessibility was explained by enhanced meaning in life among female, but not male, participants (Study 3). Taken together, these findings provide evidence that meaning in life functions as a mechanism underlying the protective role of positive romantic relationships against death-thought accessibility. Our analyses also highlight gender differences in how romantic relationships buffer against death anxiety.
articles.keywords: Romance; Psychology; Meaning; Terror management theory; Death anxiety; Anxiety; Existentialism; Mediation; Association
articles.summary: 本研究基于恐怖管理理论，通过三个实验考察了积极浪漫关系如何减少死亡焦虑。结果发现，积极浪漫关系能够增强生命意义，进而降低死亡思维可及性，且这种中介效应在女性参与者中显著，而在男性中不显著。研究揭示了生命意义作为浪漫关系缓冲死亡焦虑的机制，并突出了性别差异。

articles.title: Retributive Sentiments Track Both Deterrent and Compensatory Concerns in a Small-Scale Society and a WEIRD Sample
articles.abstract: Evolutionary theories suggest that retributive sentiments evolved to deter antisocial behavior, yet psychological evidence shows punitive sentiments imperfectly optimize deterrence. Recent theories resolve this paradox, suggesting that retributive sentiments serve to restore mutual benefit between partners by both compensating the victim and imposing an additional deterrent cost on the transgressor. In four experiments, we tested predictions of this hypothesis with adults from a small-scale, politically decentralized society (Mentawai horticulturalists, Indonesia; N = 74) and a large-scale society (the United States; N = 600). Consistent with compensatory concerns, participants demanded that victims be rewarded in the currency of their loss and that more severe punishment be imposed when victims suffered more, holding aggressor benefit constant. Consistent with compensation being entangled with deterrence, participants endorsed additional penalties beyond restitution that were higher for recidivism and intentional offenses. This proximate entanglement of compensation and deterrence helps resolve apparent contradictions about the function of punishment in humans.
articles.keywords: compensation; justice; punishment; retributivism; small-scale societies
articles.summary: 本研究通过跨文化实验探讨了报复性情感在小型社会（印度尼西亚Mentawai社会）和美国样本中的功能，发现参与者要求受害者获得损失补偿，并根据受害程度施加更严厉惩罚，同时支持超出补偿的额外惩罚以威慑累犯和故意行为。这表明补偿和威慑关注在惩罚决策中紧密纠缠，为惩罚的情感进化理论提供了新见解。

articles.title: Growing Technological Opacity and the Social Brain
articles.abstract: Thanks to our remarkable ability to transmit technical content, our technologies have become more sophisticated. Intuitively, one might assume that this evolution has imposed greater demands on the technical brain. However, recent neuroscientific research suggests that this evolution has also increasingly engaged the social brain to address the opacity it has generated in making, transactive, and use processes. Here, we build on these findings to design a neurocognitive framework that outlines the role of the social brain in (a) facilitating the transmission of making processes, (b) relying on human experts as extensions of our technical cognition, and (c) engaging with certain technologies—including machines—as if they were intentional biological agents. The framework emphasizes the dynamic interplay between the technical and social brain and explores the mechanisms that drive switching between these networks in response to technological opacity, including bottom-up perceptual cues and causal uncertainty. It also considers how expertise modulates network engagement and guides the allocation of cognitive resources. Overall, this framework provides a unified perspective on how humans navigate complex technological environments, illustrating the coevolution of technical and social cognition and the adaptive strategies that allow us to interact with technologies that we cannot fully understand.
articles.keywords: machine; technical cognition; technological evolution; transactive cognition
articles.summary: 本研究提出一个神经认知框架，探讨技术不透明性如何促使社交大脑参与，以解释人类在制造、交互和使用复杂技术过程中的适应性策略，强调技术认知与社交认知之间的协同演化与动态互动。
